Although there are signs indiclt-
Ing a gradual resumption of bust
ness and the consequent easing Of
the labor market after the semi
annual dividends are distributed,
the closing of the Republic Iron &
Steel plants and a number of
Independent concerns had a de-
pressing effect.


The Republic and it's independ-
ent allies. spurred partly by the
competition of the. United States
Steel corporation, are demanding
a Is per cent reduction in wages.
and the Amalgamated Association
of Iron and Steel Workers is strug-
Cling hard to NEE this bear on
slaught. The association insists
that dinner pals should remain as
full as last year, because prices of
bread and butter and meat and
other necessities either have ad-
vanced Or remain stationary.


The iron and steel workers are
being encouraged by other indus
trialists, who point out that the
accepting of an Is per cent cut in
wages by 10000 textile workers,
the last of whom returned to work
the past week, and a strong ten
dency to demoralize the labor mar
ket and that heroic efforts will be
required to check the bear raid and
a possible widespread panic in
wages.


A disquieting condition has de-
veloped on the Pacific coast, where
shipbuilding employees in the yards
at Grays harbor have suspended
operations because the companies
demanded the open shop, which
the employees interpret as an open
market, unfair competition and re-
sultant lower wages.


On the lower lakes labor condi
tons remain in chaotic cOndi-
ton. Stockades have been erected
